# ANALYSIS: LEVEL_REGIME_VIX_BREAKDOWN

**Filed:** 2026-06-17  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** analysis  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

The IS average P&L of -$390/trade for LEVEL-tier trades (level_rejection or level_reclaim without SUPER/ELITE upgrade) in BEARISH_REVERSAL is driven by losses in a specific VIX regime. If VIX<17.30 trades are significantly unprofitable while VIX>=17.30 trades are profitable or less losing, this would suggest the existing VIX_BEAR_THRESHOLD (if active) already mitigates the worst bucket without explicit LEVEL-tier targeting.

## Mechanism

Classify each of the 33 LEVEL-tier trades in the BEARISH_REVERSAL IS period by the VIX value at entry into four buckets:  
(A) VIX < 17.30 (flat)  
(B) 17.30 ≤ VIX < 20 (normal)  
(C) 20 ≤ VIX < 30 (elevated)  
(D) VIX ≥ 30 (crisis)  
For each bucket, compute the count of trades and average P&L. This requires a Stage-1 backtest that logs VIX at entry for every LEVEL-tier trigger in BEARISH_REVERSAL IS.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** Not applicable (diagnostic analysis, no trades proposed).
2. **Sample bias:** Sample = 33 LEVEL-tier trades in BEARISH_REVERSAL IS (source-of-truth J-day aligned IS period). Selection: all level_rejection/level_reclaim trades without SUPER/ELITE upgrade. Overfit risk: low (descriptive, no optimization). However, the IS period may not be representative of OOS if regime shifts occur.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (analysis is IS-only; OOS validation required to assess robustness).
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (P&L values must be validated against realistic OPRA simulation for top-3 J days to ensure accuracy).
5. **Failure modes:**  
   - VIX timestamp misalignment (using delayed or forward-filled VIX).  
   - Misclassification of LEVEL-tier trades (incorrectly including SUPER/ELITE upgrades).  
   - IS period definition drift (if BEARISH_REVERSAL IS window differs from J-day anchor days).  
   - Small sample size per bucket (e.g., bucket D may have very few trades, increasing variance).
6. **Concentration:** Not applicable (we are analyzing the full set of 33 trades; concentration disclosure would be relevant only if we proposed a strategy change).

## Pre-merge gate

To validate this analysis, the following must pass:  
- Stage-1 backtest that outputs VIX-at-entry and P&L for every LEVEL-tier trade in BEARISH_REVERSAL IS.  
- Gym validators confirming no regression on J-day anchor days (if any behavior change were proposed; here, none).  
- Walk-forward OOS test showing similar VIX-regime P&L patterns (if OOS data available).  
- Real-fills validation on top-3 J days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04) showing P&L within ±20% of BS-sim.

## Confidence

2 / 10 -- Cannot compute numbers without Stage-1 backtest. Confidence will increase once VIX-at-entry and P&L data for the 33 trades are available.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This analysis complements all candidates by providing diagnostic insight into BEARISH_REVERSAL performance. It conflicts with none and may inform future VIX-gated candidates (e.g., if VIX<17.30 is the loss driver, candidates targeting that regime may be redundant with existing VIX_BEAR_THRESHOLD).
